Configurations
  • Window Manager
  • dwl
  • View page source

dwl

This section documents the configuration for dwl and related tools.

Overview

This setup includes:

  • Core dwl configuration

  • Custom patches

  • Status bar (dwlb)

  • Menu system (bemenu)

  • Notification daemon (mako)

  • Keyboard customization (XKB)

Version

The tools and dependencies used get their exact version stored here:

dwl/VERSIONS

 1bemenu: bemenu v0.6.23
 2cliphist: version	0.7.0
 3dwl: dwl v0.7-dirty
 4dwlb: dwlb 0.2
 5fcft: 3.3.3
 6foot: foot version: 1.26.1-9-g6183d1b7 (Apr 28 2026, branch 'master') -pgo +ime +graphemes +toplevel-tag +blur -assertions
 7grim: 1.5.0
 8j4-dmenu-desktop: r3.2-14-gd75d640
 9mako: 1.11.0
10slurp: 1.5.0
11wlroots: 0.18.2
12xsel: xsel version 1.2.1 by Conrad Parker <conrad@vergenet.net>
13Generated: Tue Apr 28 06:52:19 AM -03 2026

Note

That file gets generated by a script and shouldn’t be edited manually.

Configuration

config.h

The main configuration file for dwl.

  • Managed as a symlink

  • Typically located at:

    /usr/local/src/dwl/config.h

  1/* Taken from https://github.com/djpohly/dwl/issues/466 */
  2#define COLOR(hex)                                                             \
  3  {((hex >> 24) & 0xFF) / 255.0f, ((hex >> 16) & 0xFF) / 255.0f,               \
  4   ((hex >> 8) & 0xFF) / 255.0f, (hex & 0xFF) / 255.0f}
  5/* appearance */
  6static const int sloppyfocus = 1; /* focus follows mouse */
  7static const int bypass_surface_visibility =
  8    0; /* 1 means idle inhibitors will disable idle tracking even if it's
  9          surface isn't visible  */
 10static const unsigned int borderpx = 1; /* border pixel of windows */
 11static const float rootcolor[] = COLOR(0xf5f5f5ff);   // background/root
 12static const float bordercolor[] = COLOR(0xddddddff); // unfocused border
 13static const float focuscolor[] = COLOR(0xee0000ff);  // focused border (RH red)
 14static const float urgentcolor[] = COLOR(0xff3b30ff); // urgent border
 15/* This conforms to the xdg-protocol. Set the alpha to zero to restore the old
 16 * behavior */
 17static const float fullscreen_bg[] = {0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f,
 18                                      1.0f}; /* You can also use glsl colors */
 19
 20/* tagging - TAGCOUNT must be no greater than 31 */
 21#define TAGCOUNT (9)
 22
 23/* logging */
 24static int log_level = WLR_ERROR;
 25
 26static const Rule rules[] = {
 27    /* app_id          		title   tags mask  isfloating  monitor   x     y */
 28    {"Gimp_EXAMPLE",   		NULL,    0,         1,          -1,      -1,  -1}, /* floating, centered */
 29    {"firefox_EXAMPLE",		NULL,    1 << 8,    0,          -1,       0,   0},
 30    /* default/example rule: can be changed but cannot be eliminated; at least
 31       one rule must exist */
 32    {"waterfox",       		NULL,    1 << 0,    0,          -1,       0,   0},
 33    {"foot",           		NULL,    1 << 1,    0,          -1,       0,   0},
 34    {"acme",           		NULL,    1 << 2,    0,          -1,       0,   0},
 35    {"spotify",        		NULL,    1 << 3,    0,          -1,       0,   0},
 36    {"org.gnome.Nautilus",  NULL,1 << 4,    0,          -1,       0,   0},
 37    {"mpv",            		NULL,    0,         1,          -1,      -1,  -1}, /* floating, centered */
 38};
 39
 40/* layout(s) */
 41static const Layout layouts[] = {
 42    /* symbol     arrange function */
 43    {"[]=", tile},
 44    {"><>", NULL}, /* no layout function means floating behavior */
 45    {"[M]", monocle},
 46};
 47
 48/* monitors */
 49/* (x=-1, y=-1) is reserved as an "autoconfigure" monitor position indicator
 50 * WARNING: negative values other than (-1, -1) cause problems with Xwayland
 51 * clients due to https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/xorg/xserver/-/issues/899 */
 52static const MonitorRule monrules[] = {
 53    /* name        mfact  nmaster scale layout       rotate/reflect x    y
 54     * example of a HiDPI laptop monitor:
 55     { "eDP-1",    0.5f,  1,      2,    &layouts[0], WL_OUTPUT_TRANSFORM_NORMAL,
 56     -1,  -1 }, */
 57    {NULL, 0.55f, 1, 1, &layouts[0], WL_OUTPUT_TRANSFORM_NORMAL, -1, -1},
 58    /* default monitor rule: can be changed but cannot be eliminated; at least
 59       one monitor rule must exist */
 60};
 61
 62/* keyboard */
 63static const struct xkb_rule_names xkb_rules = {
 64    /* can specify fields: rules, model, layout, variant, options */
 65    .options = "custom:nodead_circumflex_grave",
 66    .model = "pc105",
 67    .layout = "latam,ru",
 68};
 69
 70static const int repeat_rate = 25;
 71static const int repeat_delay = 600;
 72
 73/* Trackpad */
 74static const int tap_to_click = 1;
 75static const int tap_and_drag = 1;
 76static const int drag_lock = 1;
 77static const int natural_scrolling = 0;
 78static const int disable_while_typing = 1;
 79static const int left_handed = 0;
 80static const int middle_button_emulation = 0;
 81/* You can choose between:
 82LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SCROLL_NO_SCROLL
 83LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SCROLL_2FG
 84LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SCROLL_EDGE
 85LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SCROLL_ON_BUTTON_DOWN
 86*/
 87static const enum libinput_config_scroll_method scroll_method =
 88    LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SCROLL_2FG;
 89
 90/* You can choose between:
 91LIBINPUT_CONFIG_CLICK_METHOD_NONE
 92LIBINPUT_CONFIG_CLICK_METHOD_BUTTON_AREAS
 93LIBINPUT_CONFIG_CLICK_METHOD_CLICKFINGER
 94*/
 95static const enum libinput_config_click_method click_method =
 96    LIBINPUT_CONFIG_CLICK_METHOD_BUTTON_AREAS;
 97
 98/* You can choose between:
 99LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SEND_EVENTS_ENABLED
100LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SEND_EVENTS_DISABLED
101LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SEND_EVENTS_DISABLED_ON_EXTERNAL_MOUSE
102*/
103static const uint32_t send_events_mode = LIBINPUT_CONFIG_SEND_EVENTS_ENABLED;
104
105/* You can choose between:
106LIBINPUT_CONFIG_ACCEL_PROFILE_FLAT
107LIBINPUT_CONFIG_ACCEL_PROFILE_ADAPTIVE
108*/
109static const enum libinput_config_accel_profile accel_profile =
110    LIBINPUT_CONFIG_ACCEL_PROFILE_ADAPTIVE;
111static const double accel_speed = 0.0;
112
113/* You can choose between:
114LIBINPUT_CONFIG_TAP_MAP_LRM -- 1/2/3 finger tap maps to left/right/middle
115LIBINPUT_CONFIG_TAP_MAP_LMR -- 1/2/3 finger tap maps to left/middle/right
116*/
117static const enum libinput_config_tap_button_map button_map =
118    LIBINPUT_CONFIG_TAP_MAP_LRM;
119
120/* If you want to use the windows key for MODKEY, use WLR_MODIFIER_LOGO */
121#define MODKEY WLR_MODIFIER_LOGO
122
123#define TAGKEYS(KEY, SKEY, TAG)                                                \
124  {MODKEY, KEY, view, {.ui = 1 << TAG}},                                       \
125      {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_CTRL, KEY, toggleview, {.ui = 1 << TAG}},         \
126      {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT, SKEY, tag, {.ui = 1 << TAG}}, {            \
127    MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_CTRL | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT, SKEY, toggletag, {        \
128      .ui = 1 << TAG                                                           \
129    }                                                                          \
130  }
131
132/* helper for spawning shell commands in the pre dwm-5.0 fashion */
133#define SHCMD(cmd)                                                             \
134  {                                                                            \
135    .v = (const char *[]) { "/bin/sh", "-c", cmd, NULL }                       \
136  }
137
138/* commands */
139static const char *debugcmd[] = {"/bin/sh", "-c", "env > /tmp/dwl-env.txt",
140                                 NULL};
141static const char *termcmd[] = {"foot", NULL};
142static const char *appcmd[] = {
143    "j4-dmenu-desktop",
144    "--dmenu=/home/mnlcz/Projects/config/wm/dwl/bemenu/bemenu-themed",
145    NULL};
146static const char *menucmd[] = {
147    "/home/mnlcz/Projects/config/wm/dwl/bemenu/bemenu-run-themed", NULL};
148static const char *screenshotcmd[] = {
149    "/bin/sh", "-c",
150    "grim -g \"$(slurp)\" ~/Pictures/Screenshots/screenshot-$(date "
151    "+%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).png",
152    NULL};
153static const char *screenshotfullcmd[] = {
154    "/bin/sh", "-c",
155    "grim ~/Pictures/Screenshots/screenshot-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).png", NULL};
156static const char *cliphist[] = {
157    "/bin/sh", "-c",
158    "cliphist list | "
159    "/home/mnlcz/Projects/config/wm/dwl/bemenu/bemenu-themed -p "
160    "'clipboard:' | cliphist decode | wl-copy",
161    NULL};
162
163static const Key keys[] = {
164    /* Note that Shift changes certain key codes: 2 -> at, etc. */
165    /* modifier                  key                  function          argument
166     */
167    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_CTRL, XKB_KEY_d, spawn, {.v = debugcmd}},
168    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_p, spawn, {.v = appcmd}},
169    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT, XKB_KEY_P, spawn, {.v = menucmd}},
170    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT, XKB_KEY_Return, spawn, {.v = termcmd}},
171    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_j, focusstack, {.i = +1}},
172    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_k, focusstack, {.i = -1}},
173    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_i, incnmaster, {.i = +1}},
174    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_d, incnmaster, {.i = -1}},
175    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_h, setmfact, {.f = -0.05f}},
176    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_l, setmfact, {.f = +0.05f}},
177    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_Return, zoom, {0}},
178    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_Tab, view, {0}},
179    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_q, killclient, {0}},
180    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_t, setlayout, {.v = &layouts[0]}},
181    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_f, setlayout, {.v = &layouts[1]}},
182    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_m, setlayout, {.v = &layouts[2]}},
183    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_space, setlayout, {0}},
184    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT, XKB_KEY_space, togglefloating, {0}},
185    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_e, togglefullscreen, {0}},
186    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_0, view, {.ui = ~0}},
187    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT, XKB_KEY_parenright, tag, {.ui = ~0}},
188    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_comma, focusmon, {.i = WLR_DIRECTION_LEFT}},
189    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_period, focusmon, {.i = WLR_DIRECTION_RIGHT}},
190    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT,
191     XKB_KEY_less,
192     tagmon,
193     {.i = WLR_DIRECTION_LEFT}},
194    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT,
195     XKB_KEY_greater,
196     tagmon,
197     {.i = WLR_DIRECTION_RIGHT}},
198    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_1, XKB_KEY_exclam, 0),
199    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_2, XKB_KEY_at, 1),
200    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_3, XKB_KEY_numbersign, 2),
201    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_4, XKB_KEY_dollar, 3),
202    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_5, XKB_KEY_percent, 4),
203    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_6, XKB_KEY_asciicircum, 5),
204    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_7, XKB_KEY_ampersand, 6),
205    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_8, XKB_KEY_asterisk, 7),
206    TAGKEYS(XKB_KEY_9, XKB_KEY_parenleft, 8),
207    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT, XKB_KEY_Q, quit, {0}},
208    {0, XKB_KEY_Print, spawn, {.v = screenshotfullcmd}},
209    {MODKEY | WLR_MODIFIER_SHIFT, XKB_KEY_S, spawn, {.v = screenshotcmd}},
210    {MODKEY, XKB_KEY_v, spawn, {.v = cliphist}},
211    {0, XKB_KEY_F1, switchlayout, {.ui = 0}},
212    {0, XKB_KEY_F2, switchlayout, {.ui = 1}},
213
214    /* Ctrl-Alt-Backspace and Ctrl-Alt-Fx used to be handled by X server */
215    {WLR_MODIFIER_CTRL | WLR_MODIFIER_ALT, XKB_KEY_Terminate_Server, quit, {0}},
216/* Ctrl-Alt-Fx is used to switch to another VT, if you don't know what a VT is
217 * do not remove them.
218 */
219#define CHVT(n)                                                                \
220  {                                                                            \
221    WLR_MODIFIER_CTRL | WLR_MODIFIER_ALT, XKB_KEY_XF86Switch_VT_##n, chvt, {   \
222      .ui = (n)                                                                \
223    }                                                                          \
224  }
225    CHVT(1),
226    CHVT(2),
227    CHVT(3),
228    CHVT(4),
229    CHVT(5),
230    CHVT(6),
231    CHVT(7),
232    CHVT(8),
233    CHVT(9),
234    CHVT(10),
235    CHVT(11),
236    CHVT(12),
237};
238
239static const Button buttons[] = {
240    {MODKEY, BTN_LEFT, moveresize, {.ui = CurMove}},
241    {MODKEY, BTN_MIDDLE, togglefloating, {0}},
242    {MODKEY, BTN_RIGHT, moveresize, {.ui = CurResize}},
243};

Note

Changes require recompiling dwl.

dwl.desktop

The desktop entry for dwl.

  • Typically symlinked to:

    /usr/local/share/wayland-sessions/dwl.desktop

Warning

The dwl’s Makefile overwrites the desktop entry on each compilation, there are two ways of solving this:

  1. Remove the .desktop operations in the Makefile.

  2. Symlink the provided .desktop to /usr/local/src/dwl/dwl.desktop, so the Makefile uses it instead of the default.

Patches

Core patches are tracked in:

  • dwl/PATCHES

Custom patches (maintained locally):

  • floating-window-pos.patch

  • switchlayout.patch

Key Features

switchlayout.patch

  • Adds programmatic layout switching

  • Writes current layout to:

    /tmp/dwl-layout

floating-window-pos.patch

  • Extends Rule with x and y

  • Allows precise floating window placement

Status Bar (dwlb)

Configuration located in:

  • wm/dwl/dwlb/

Includes:

  • config.h (symlinked)

  • status.sh (status generator)

  • Custom patches (e.g. no-flicker.patch)

status.sh

Generates the status bar content.

#!/bin/sh

get_layout() {
    cat /tmp/dwl-layout 2>/dev/null || echo "LATAM"
}

get_network() {
    if ip route show default 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
        echo "󰈀"
    else
        echo "󰈁"
    fi
}

get_memory() {
    free -h | awk '/Mem:/ {print $3"/"$2}'
}

get_clock() {
    date '+%a %d %b %H:%M'
}

while true; do
    dwlb -status HDMI-A-1 "^fg(ee0000)$(get_network)  $(get_memory)  $(get_layout)  $(get_clock)^fg()"
    sleep 1
done

Menu (bemenu)

Configuration located in:

  • wm/dwl/bemenu/

Components:

  • bemenu-themed: defines colors and fonts

  • bemenu-run-themed: symlink wrapper

Purpose:

  • Avoid hardcoding style in config.h

  • Centralize appearance configuration

Startup

The entry point for dwl is:

  • dwl-startup

Used by:

  • dwl.desktop

Responsibilities:

  • Initialize environment

  • Launch required services

  • Start dwl session

Scripts

Utility scripts include:

-log.sh

  • dwl-patches-log.sh

  • dwlb-patches-log.sh

  • dwl-version-log.sh

Purpose:

  • Populate PATCHES and VERSIONS

  • Track applied patches and tool versions

Keyboard (XKB)

Custom symbols

Defined in:

  • wm/dwl/xkb/custom

Symlinked to:

  • /usr/share/X11/xkb/symbols/custom

Configuration

Requires adding to:

/usr/share/X11/xkb/rules/evdev

custom:nodead_circumflex_grave = +custom(nodead_circumflex_grave)

Behavior

  • AltGr+{ -> ^

  • AltGr+} -> backtick

  • Spanish tilde remains a dead key

Notification Daemon

Configured via:

  • wm/dwl/mako/config

Controls:

  • Notification appearance

  • Behavior

Integration

This setup interacts with:

  • Terminal

  • Shells

  • Scripts

  • MPV

Notes

  • Configuration files are symlinked into system paths.

  • Patches must be reapplied when updating dwl.

  • Misconfigured symlinks can break builds or runtime behavior.

Previous Next
